articles.title: Prayagraj, Varanasi among nation’s hottest as heatwave returns | Hindustan Times - Hindustan Times
articles.full_text:
Heatwave conditions resurfaced in parts of eastern Uttar Pradesh on Wednesday, pushing Prayagraj and Varanasi to the top of the country’s temperature chart for the second consecutive day. However, evening showers, gusty winds and overcast skies brought much-needed relief from the scorching heat.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) reported heatwave conditions at isolated places in Varanasi, Prayagraj, Churk (Sonbhadra) and Bahraich as temperatures soared across eastern Uttar Pradesh.
Prayagraj remained the hottest city in the country with a maximum temperature of 45°C, while Varanasi was the second hottest at 42.8°C. On Tuesday, the two cities had recorded 44°C and 43°C respectively.
After witnessing another scorching day, Prayagraj experienced a change in weather during the second half of Wednesday as clouds gathered and dust-laden winds swept across the city. The Sangam city recorded 4.6 mm of rainfall, providing relief from the intense heat.
Varanasi also witnessed light rainfall on Wednesday evening after three days of oppressive heat. Gusty winds accompanied the rain, which continued for around 20 minutes. The maximum temperature at Varanasi BHU was recorded at 42.8°C, while Varanasi airport registered 41.5°C.
At Prayagraj, weather expert and former head of the geography department at Allahabad University, Prof BN Mishra, said the Arabian Sea branch of the monsoon has already advanced into parts of Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Telangana, while the Bay of Bengal branch has reached coastal Odisha.
{{/usCountry}}At Prayagraj, weather expert and former head of the geography department at Allahabad University, Prof BN Mishra, said the Arabian Sea branch of the monsoon has already advanced into parts of Madhya Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Telangana, while the Bay of Bengal branch has reached coastal Odisha.
{{/usCountry}}He said that if light rainfall accompanied by winds of 50-60 kmph occurs in Prayagraj and adjoining areas within the next 24 to 48 hours, it would help draw moisture-laden winds from central India towards the Ganga plains, paving the way for the monsoon’s arrival in the region.
However, he cautioned that if such conditions do not develop soon, the arrival of the monsoon in Prayagraj could be delayed by another four to five days.
Prof Mishra noted that Prayagraj and neighbouring districts lie in a crucial zone of the Ganga plains where the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal branches of the monsoon converge. Given the prevailing overcast conditions and drop in temperature, he said there is a strong possibility that the monsoon is already setting in.
Other major temperatures recorded in the state included Jhansi and Churk at 42°C, Kanpur IAF at 41.6°C, Bahraich at 41.4°C, Hamirpur at 41.2°C and Banda at 41°C.
In Lucknow, the maximum and minimum temperatures were recorded at 39.6°C and 29.6°C respectively. The weather office has forecast mainly clear skies on Thursday, with temperatures likely to hover around 41°C and 30°C.

articles.title: Mercury touches 45°C in Uttar Pradesh: Heatwave warning for 26 districts, rain possible in 20 cities; winds... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Mercury touches 45°C in Uttar Pradesh:Heatwave warning for 26 districts, rain possible in 20 cities; winds may reach 50 kmph
Uttar Pradesh's weather is experiencing continuous fluctuations. While some areas are experiencing intense heat, others are witnessing rain accompanied by strong winds. On Thursday, there is a rain alert in 20 districts and a heatwave alert in 26 districts. During this period, winds may blow at speeds of up to 50 km/h in some places.
The heat has increased once again. In the last 24 hours, Prayagraj remained the hottest city in the state. The temperature here was recorded at 45 °C. On Wednesday, Varanasi experienced heavy rainfall. Roads were waterlogged. Prayagraj, Jhansi, and Gorakhpur also received rain.
Lucknow's meteorologist, Atul Singh, stated-
The monsoon has reached near Maharajganj district on the Bihar border. It is expected that the monsoon may enter UP via Sonbhadra between June 23 and 25. The normal date for the monsoon's arrival in UP is considered to be between June 18 and 20. This means there is a delay of 4 to 5 days this time.
How will the weather be for the next 5 days
- June 18: There will be cloud movement across the entire state. Rain may occur in some places.
- June 19: Light to heavy rain may occur across the entire state. Storms are also possible in some places.
- June 20: Most districts of the state will remain cloudy. Heavy rain may occur in some places.
- June 21: The weather will remain clear across the entire state. Strong winds will blow. Drizzle may occur in some places due to cloud movement.
- June 22: Most districts of the state will remain cloudy. Intense heavy rain may occur in some places.

articles.title: UP Weather: No Relief For Next 4-5 Days As IMD Predicts Heatwave Conditions In THESE Districts | Check Forecast - The Daily Jagran
articles.full_text:
- IMD warns of heatwave conditions for next four to five days.
- Temperatures to exceed 40°C in 26 Uttar Pradesh districts.
- Public advised to limit outdoor activities, stay hydrated.
Jagran Correspondent, Lucknow: No immediate relief is in sight as a lack of active weather systems across the state is expected to trigger a sharp rise in temperatures. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has warned that heatwave conditions are likely to grip several areas over the next four to five days, with maximum temperatures set to rise across 26 districts.
The weather office has advised the public to limit outdoor activities during peak afternoon hours, stay hydrated, and monitor official weather updates closely.
Heatwave Likely In THESE Districts
According to Meteorologist Atul Kumar Singh, heatwave conditions will prevail across several regions, including Banda, Chitrakoot, Kaushambi, Prayagraj, Fatehpur, Pratapgarh, Sonbhadra, Mirzapur, Chandauli, Varanasi, Sant Ravidas Nagar, Jaunpur, Ghazipur, and Azamgarh.
Similar conditions are likely in Gonda, Balrampur, Shravasti, Bahraich, Lakhimpur Kheri, Sitapur, Barabanki, Rae Bareli, Amethi, Sultanpur, Ayodhya, and Ambedkar Nagar.
Temperature To Cross 40 Degrees In Several Districts
Temperatures are set to rise sharply across the state, with the maximum temperature in several districts likely to reach or exceed 40 degrees Celsius. The meteorological department has warned that the weather situation will intensify by Friday, with the number of heatwave-affected districts expected to rise to 45.
This includes most districts in the Awadh region, while several other districts across eastern and western Uttar Pradesh will experience maximum temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius.
Uttar Pradesh Weather Update
On Wednesday, strong winds swept across several districts of Uttar Pradesh, including Lucknow, triggering a slight dip in temperatures. Despite this, Lucknow's daytime maximum settled at 39.6 degrees Celsius, 1.6 degrees Celsius above normal.
Meanwhile, Prayagraj once again emerged as the hottest city in the state, recording a scorching high of 45 degrees Celsius. Varanasi recorded a maximum of 42.8 degrees Celsius, followed closely by Sonbhadra and Jhansi at 42 degrees Celsius each, Hamirpur at 41.2 degrees Celsius, and Banda at 41 degrees Celsius.
(With Inputs From Jagran.com)

articles.title: UP Weather: यूपी में तेज धूप ने बढ़ाई गर्मी, आज प्रयागराज समेत 29 जिलों में हीटवेव का अलर्ट, - ABP News
articles.search_term: (लू OR "लू चलना" OR "भीषण गर्मी" OR "प्रचंड गर्मी" OR "कड़ी धूप" OR "तेज धूप" OR "गर्म हवा" OR "उष्ण लहर" OR "ताप लहर" OR "हीट वेव" OR "झुलसाने वाली गर्मी" OR "तपती गर्मी" OR "भयंकर गर्मी" OR "गर्मी का कहर" OR "गर्मी का प्रकोप" OR "अत्यधिक गर्मी" OR मौसम OR "मौसम अपडेट" OR "मौसम का हाल" OR "मौसम पूर्वानुमान" OR "मौसम अलर्ट" OR आईएमडी OR "आईएमडी पूर्वानुमान" OR "आईएमडी अलर्ट" OR "तापमान में बढ़ोतरी" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR "गर्मी से निजात") Uttar Pradesh
articles.full_text:
UP Weather: यूपी में तेज धूप ने बढ़ाई गर्मी, आज प्रयागराज समेत 29 जिलों में हीटवेव का अलर्ट, जानें- आपके शहर का मौसम
UP Weather: उत्तर प्रदेश के ज्यादातर जिलों में आज मौसम शुष्क रहने का अनुमान जताया गया है. इस दौरान वाराणसी-प्रयागराज समेत कई जिलों में भीषण लू की चेतावनी है, जिससे लोगों को परेशानी होगी.
उत्तर प्रदेश में धीरे-धीरे पारा चढ़ रहा है और लोगों को तेज धूप व उमस भरी गर्मी का सामना करना पड़ रहा है. वहीं दूसरी तरफ कुछ इलाकों में हल्की-फुल्की बूंदाबांदी का भी मौसम बना हुआ है. लेकिन, इससे गर्मी से राहत नहीं हैं. मौसम विभाग ने आज 18 जून को प्रयागराज-वाराणसी समेत 20 जिलों में गरज-चमक के साथ बारिश होने का अनुमान जताया है. जबकि 29 जिलों में हीटवेच की भी चेतावनी दी गई है.
यूपी में एक बार फिर से मौसम बदल रहा है, जिसके चलते लोगों को गर्मी से राहत के आसार नहीं हैं. मौसम विभाग ने आज राज्य के कई हिस्सों में लू की चेतावनी दी हैं. पूर्वांचल से लेकर बुंदेलखंड और अवध के जिलों में आज सूरज की तपिश से बुरा हाल रहेगा. सबसे ज्यादा गर्मी बांदा, झांसी, प्रयागराज, वाराणसी, हमीरपुर, सोनभद्र व आसपास के इलाकों में रहेगी. वहीं कुछ जिलों में कहीं-कहीं बूंदाबांदी भी हो सकती हैं लेकिन इससे तापमान पर कोई असर नहीं पड़ेगा.
इन जिलों में गरज-चमक के साथ बूंदाबांदी
यूपी में सहारनपुर, शामली और मुजफ्फरनगर, प्रयागराज, वाराणसी, गाज़ीपुर, चंदौली, मीरजापुर, सोनभद्र, जौनपुर, संत रविदास नगर, प्रतापगढ़, कौशांबी, चित्रकूट, बांदा, फतेहपुर, हमीरपुर, महोबा, झांसी और ललितपुर में एक या दो जगहों पर गरज-चमक के साथ बारिश की बौछारें पड़ने की संभावना जताई गई है.
प्रयागराज-वाराणसी में हीट वेव की चेतावनी
वहीं सीतापुर, लखीमपुर खीरी, बहराइच, श्रावस्ती, बाराबंकी, अयोध्या, गोंडा, अमेठी, रायबरेली, फतेहपुर, बांदा, चित्रकूट, कौशांबी, प्रतापगढ़, प्रयागराज, वाराणसी, गाज़ीपुर, चंदौली, मीरजापुर, सोनभद्र, जौनपुर, संत रविदास नगर, प्रतापगढ़, कौशांबी, जौनपुर, आजमगढ़, अंबेडकर नगर, सुल्तानपुर और बलरामपुर में आज हीट वेव का यलो अलर्ट दिया गया है.
राजधानी लखनऊ में भी आज मौसम शुष्क रहेगा और तेज धूप के साथ उमस रहेगी जबकि सीतापुर, अयोध्या आसपास के इलाकों में भी लोगों को प्रचंड गर्मी का सामना करना पड़ेगा. इन जगहों पर आज अधिकतम तापमान 38-39 डिग्री के आसपास रह सकता है. वहीं नोएडा, गाजियाबाद, मेरठ, आगरा और मथुरा में भी पारा हाई रहेगा. तेज धूप के चलते उमस भरी गर्मी बनी रहेगी. शाम के समय कही-कहीं धूलभरी आंधी चल सकती है.
